channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Jim Acosta Analyzes Poll Showing Dr. Annie Andrews Closing In On Lindsey Graham Amid His Death
item.description: U.S. Senator Lindsey Graham (R-SC) died Saturday night from a sudden and unexpected illness, with his office citing a 'brief and sudden illness' as the cause. Hours before his medical incident, a vira...

item.title: Pakistan Urges Restraint Amid Middle East Escalation to Promote Regional Peace
item.description: Pakistan has expressed 'deep concern' over the escalating tensions in the Middle East following recent attacks by the US and Iran, with Gulf countries also facing strikes. The Foreign Office reiterate...

item.title: Trump and Netanyahu Face Criticism for Leveraging Lindsey Graham's Death to Advance Political Agendas
item.description: The article details the backlash against U.S. President Donald Trump and Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu for capitalizing on the sudden death of Senator Lindsey Graham (R-SC) to push their p...

item.title: Bangladesh Faces Severe Monsoon Impact: 50 Lives Lost, Thousands Displaced
item.description: Bangladesh is grappling with devastating monsoon floods and landslides that have claimed at least 50 lives and displaced tens of thousands of people. The crisis has hit the southeastern districts hard...

item.title: Devon and Cornwall Police Investigate Ann Widdecombe Murder, Find No Political Motive
item.description: Police investigating the alleged murder of Ann Widdecombe have stated there is 'nothing to suggest it was politically motivated'. Devon and Cornwall Police confirmed they are not seeking additional su...

item.title: 'Where's My Groom?' review: Joy Isi Bewaji and Nene Aliemeke deliver a powerful one-woman stage performance on betrayal and womanhood
item.description: Where's My Groom? is an intimate one-woman stage play written and directed by Joy Isi Bewaji, with Nene Aliemeke carrying the entire performance from beginning to end. The production tells the story o...

item.title: New standards for universities to combat racism and antisemitism
item.description: The Albanese government has introduced new mandatory standards requiring Australian universities to address racism and antisemitism, with vice chancellors facing scrutiny by a royal commission. The re...

item.title: VCK Party Maintains Strength Amid Internal Challenges
item.description: The article discusses the stance of VCK leader Thol. Thirumavalavan regarding the party's resilience against internal dissent. In Dharmapuri, he addressed claims that some members attempted to undermi...

item.title: Makki Lucino's Mistake on "Everybody Sing" Highlights Behind-the-Scenes Moments
item.description: In a recent episode of the popular TV show "Everybody Sing," Makki Lucino, a well-known personality in the entertainment industry, made a notable mistake that became a talking point among viewers. The...

item.title: Pakistan Urges Restraint and Upholding of Islamabad MoU Amid Escalating ME Tensions
item.description: Pakistan has expressed deep concern over the recent escalation in Middle East tensions, urging all parties to exercise restraint and uphold their commitments under the Islamabad Memorandum of Understa...

item.title: Political Parties Navigate Allegations of Betrayal in Tanzania
item.description: The ruling party ACT-Wazalendo has addressed accusations of political betrayal from the opposition Chadema, highlighting tensions within Tanzania's multi-party system. The dispute underscores shifting...

item.title: Canada Combats International Extortion Ring Led by Lawrence Bishnoi
item.description: Lawrence Bishnoi, a Canadian-based leader of an international extortion ring, has been implicated in organized crime activities targeting Sikh communities and temporary workers. The FBI and RCMP have ...

item.title: Antonio Rattin's Legacy: How His 1966 World Cup Moment Shaped Modern Football
item.description: Antonio Rattin, a legendary Argentine midfielder who played a pivotal role in football history, passed away at the age of 89. His death coincides with Argentina's campaign in the 2026 World Cup, where...

item.title: Passing of Bafana Midfielder Jayden Adams Marks Loss for South African Football
item.description: The tragic passing of 25-year-old Bafana Bafana and Mamelodi Sundowns midfielder Jayden Adams has triggered a national mourning period in South Africa. Police have initiated an inquest following the d...

item.title: US Military Confirms Strait of Hormuz Traffic Remains Uninterrupted Amid Iran's Claims
item.description: The US military has confirmed that ships are still moving through the Strait of Hormuz despite Iran's claim to have closed it. CENTCOM, which oversees US forces in the Middle East, stated on X that Ir...

item.title: Even Realities G2 Smart Glasses Focus on Productivity with Camera-Free Design
item.description: Even Realities' G2 smart glasses prioritize productivity over recording capabilities, featuring a monochrome neon-style heads-up display for text and information. The device lacks cameras and speakers...

item.title: Police investigate Ann Widdecombe's murder as Nigel Farage claims car was seen before her death
item.description: The police have intensified their investigation into the murder of Ann Widdecombe, a former member of Parliament, following reports that a car was seen arriving at her home moments before her death. A...

item.title: XO Team Members: Profiles and Key Information
item.description: The article provides an in-depth look at the XO Team, a collective of social media influencers and creators based in Eastern Europe. Founded in 2020 by Gary Grey and Mary Senn, the team has grown to i...

item.title: BetPawa extends sponsorship to boost Dar es Salaam basketball league
item.description: Sports betting firm betPawa has renewed its sponsorship of the Basketball Dar es Salaam League (BDL), allocating Sh588.9 million for the 2026 season. The Locker Room Bonus (LRB) program will reward pl...

item.title: Urbanisation reshapes frog communities in Western Ghats, study finds
item.description: A study conducted in Udupi, Karnataka, reveals that urbanisation in the Western Ghats is not just causing species loss but reshaping frog communities by filtering species based on traits like body siz...
